title: quercus lanuginosa var. faginea
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. faginea, also known as the 'Faginoid' or 'Downy White Oak', is a variety of the Quercus lanuginosa tree, belonging to the Fagaceae family and native to the Himalayas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. faginea include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
